title: GitLab Merge Request Pipeline Success Rate Drop
app: GitLab Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 6848) or GitLab webhook HEC
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:pipeline" object_kind=merge_request_event OR source=merge_request
| bin _time span=1d
| eval ok=if(status="success",1,0)
| stats sum(ok) as ok count as total by project_path, _time
| eval success_pct=round(100*ok/total,1)
| where total>=20 AND success_pct < 85
| sort 0 project_path _time
| streamstats current=f last(success_pct) as prev_pct by project_path
| where isnotnull(prev_pct) AND success_pct < prev_pct - 10
description: Sudden drops in MR pipeline pass rate often precede flaky tests, runner issues, or policy changes.
value: Gives engineering leads an early signal when merge quality regresses before default-branch protections fail.
implementation: Ingest MR pipeline payloads; tune minimum daily volume. Compare 1d success rate to prior day; alert on >10pt drop.
visualization: Line chart (success_pct by project), Single value (worst project).
knownFalsePositives: Build failures spike during CI infrastructure issues, dependency registry outages, flaky test runs, or major framework upgrades.
grandmaExplanation: We watch GitLab runner and pipeline signals so broken jobs, storage, or runners do not stall your teams.
